eyebrow.es: Breakout de ciudadanía digital · Grado K
eyebrow.vi: Breakout công dân số · Lớp K
eyebrow.ur: ڈیجیٹل شہریت بریک آؤٹ · جماعت K
eyebrow.zh: 数字公民破解 · 幼儿园

chrome.sub: Stay safe and be kind on the tablet. Solve four locks. Use the picture clues to help you.
chrome.briefP: The internet is fun. It is safest when you ask a grown-up, keep your name private, and stop when something feels bad. Look at the clues. Then open all four locks. Each lock tells you why the safe, kind way is the best way.
chrome.footerText: Digital Citizenship Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winP: You know who to ask for help. You know kind from unkind. You keep your name private. You stop and ask when something feels unsafe. You are safe and kind online!

clues.title: A grown-up helps
clues.body: A trusted grown-up keeps you safe. That is a parent, a teacher, or a helper you know. Ask them when you need help.

clues.title: Kind feels good
clues.body: Kind words make a friend smile. 'Nice job!' is kind. Mean words hurt. Pick the kind way.

clues.title: Some things stay private
clues.body: Your name, your home, and your photo are private. Do not type them to people you do not know. Keep them safe.

clues.title: Stop, then tap
clues.body: Feels weird or scary? Do not tap. Stop first. Ask a grown-up. Then it is safe to play.

clues.title: When you are not sure
clues.body: If you do not know, ask. A grown-up can tell you if it is safe. Asking is always smart.

clues.title: The reading corner dino
clues.body: Our reading corner has a big green toy dino named Rex. Rex holds all the books. This is fun, but it will not open a lock.

locks.title: Who Do You Ask?
locks.q: Something online feels weird. Who is the best one to ask for help?
options: A stranger in a game chat
A trusted grown-up, like your teacher
locks.reason: A trusted grown-up you know keeps you safe and can help. A stranger might not be safe, and tapping alone can get you lost, so asking a grown-up is best.

locks.title: Kind or Unkind?

items.t: Ask a friend to play with you
Send a mean face to be rude
Tell a friend they are not welcome

locks.reason: Kind choices help friends feel good — a nice word or an invite. A mean face or telling a friend they are not welcome hurts, so we leave those unchecked.

locks.title: Keep It Safe
locks.q: Your name and your home are ______. You do not share them with people you do not know.
locks.reason: Private things stay with you and your family. Keeping your name and home private helps you stay safe online.

locks.title: Something Feels Unsafe
locks.q: Something feels scary. Tap the steps in the RIGHT order.
locks.reason: First STOP so nothing bad happens. Then ASK a trusted grown-up if it is okay. Only then is it safe to PLAY. Stop, ask, play.
